source.domain: workshop fixtures and small machine-shop accessories

requirements.part_name: Low-Profile V-Block Clamp Jaw - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Low-Profile V-Block Clamp Jaw'. The broader use
case is: Used on a milling machine vise to hold small round stock or tubing without raising the
workpiece too high above the jaws. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by:
Hardened rectangular vise jaw insert with machined V-groove, counterbored mounting holes, and edge
chamfers. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Provides a centered V-groove contact surface and clamp relief
features so cylindrical work can be gripped repeatably. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Combines prismatic jaw geometry with angled groove faces, hole placement,
symmetry, relief cuts, and chamfered working edges. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 80 mm x 65 mm x 55 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 7 mm on the length centerline at X=13 mm and X=67 mm, Y=32 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 30 mm long x 17 mm wide through the part, centered at X=40 mm, Y=32 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 21 mm x 24 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: centered V-groove
functional_features.description: Cut a 90 degree V-groove along the full X length on the top face, groove mouth 32 mm wide and depth 18 mm.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 18 degrees over the last 18 mm of length.

functional_features.name: counterbored seat
functional_features.description: Add a central counterbore diameter 25 mm x 6 mm deep around the center feature.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'centered V-groove' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'machined angled reference face' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'counterbored seat' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Low-Profile V-Block Clamp Jaw'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

source.domain: workshop fixtures and small machine-shop accessories
source.raw_idea_title: Adjustable Drill Press Depth Stop Collar

requirements.part_name: Adjustable Drill Press Depth Stop Collar - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable Drill Press Depth Stop Collar'. The
broader use case is: Accessory fitted around a drill press quill or stop rod for quickly setting
repeatable drilling depth in a small workshop. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied
by: Split cylindrical collar with clamp screw boss, radial slot, bored center hole, and knurled or
faceted grip rim. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Locks at a chosen height and provides a positive
mechanical stop for repeated hole depths. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Requires accurate cylindrical modeling, split clamp features, screw clearance, boss
integration, and small ergonomic surface details. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside,
face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No
separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 86 mm and length 108 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 28 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 38 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 28 mm wide over 100 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=36 mm and X=72 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 3 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: cross relief slot
functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 5 mm wide across the top flat at X=54 mm, depth 8 mm.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single cylindrical body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'axial through bore' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'end counterbores' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'milled reference flat' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'radial clamp holes' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'split relief slit' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'outside edge treatment' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'cross relief slot' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Round features are coaxial unless the requirement explicitly says they are radial or offset.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Adjustable Drill Press Depth Stop Collar'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

requirements.part_name: T-Slot Step Clamp Toe - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'T-Slot Step Clamp Toe'. The broader use case is: A
replacement toe piece for a milling table step-clamp set used to press down on irregular workpieces.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined clamp toe with serrated step
profile, radiused contact nose, elongated bolt slot, and hardened bearing pad. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Transfers clamping force from a strap clamp onto the workpiece while allowing height
adjustment from the stepped rear surface. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Tests stepped geometry, elongated slots, angled contact surfaces, serrations, fillets, and
load-bearing proportions. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 155 mm x 95 mm x 44 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 8 mm on the length centerline at X=19 mm and X=136 mm, Y=47 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 51 mm long x 15 mm wide through the part, centered at X=77 mm, Y=47 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 38 mm x 31 mm x 3 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: serrated contact edge
functional_features.description: Cut 12 equal triangular serrations across the rear edge, each 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 11 degrees over the last 31 mm of length.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 11 mm and undercut 4 mm for registration.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Lathe Carriage Dial Indicator Holder'. The broader
use case is: Mounts a dial indicator to a small engine lathe carriage for measuring travel or
setting repeatable stops. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Compact machined
arm with dovetail or clamp mount, indicator stem bore, pinch slot, and screw boss. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Positions an indicator stem rigidly near the work axis while allowing fine orientation
adjustment. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Involves intersecting
bores, clamp slots, compact offset arms, functional clearances, and orientation-critical reference
faces. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from
one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the

requirements.part_name: Quick-Change Toolpost Boring Bar Sleeve - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Quick-Change Toolpost Boring Bar Sleeve'. The
broader use case is: Insert sleeve used in a lathe quick-change tool holder to adapt smaller boring
bars to a larger holder bore. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Precision
cylindrical sleeve with through bore, external flat, split relief slit, and set-screw dimples. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Centers and supports a boring bar while giving set screws flat bearing
surfaces and preventing rotation. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Tests concentric cylindrical features, thin-wall part representation, flats on round stock, slots,
and small localized contact pockets. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized
using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both
ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate
inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: axial through bore

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 28 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 24 mm wide over 32 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M8 holes through the top flat at X=13 mm and X=26 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 2 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: opposed wrench flats
functional_features.description: Mill two opposed flats across the outside, leaving 64 mm across flats over the middle third of the length.

functional_features.name: cross relief slot
functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 4 mm wide across the top flat at X=20 mm, depth 7 mm.

requirements.part_name: Machinist Parallel Keeper Clip - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Machinist Parallel Keeper Clip'. The broader use
case is: Small shop accessory used to keep matched parallels paired and upright in a vise drawer or
setup tray. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Spring-style U-shaped machined
clip with relief grooves, finger tab, and soft-contact inner pads or pockets. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Holds two parallels together at their ends without damaging precision ground faces. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires thin-section geometry, internal
clearances, rounded transitions, contact pockets, and nontrivial open U-shaped topology. The part is
made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet
or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features
into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate
welded pieces.

source.raw_idea_title: Bench Grinder Tool Rest Angle Block

requirements.part_name: Bench Grinder Tool Rest Angle Block - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Bench Grinder Tool Rest Angle Block'. The broader
use case is: Fixture used on a bench grinder tool rest to quickly set a repeatable sharpening angle
for lathe tools or drill bits. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Triangular
or wedge-shaped machined block with stop lip, clamping slot, angle-engraving recess, and relieved
underside. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Provides a fixed angled reference surface that registers against
the grinder rest and supports the tool at a known angle. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Tests angled datum surfaces, wedge geometry, slots through sloped faces, small
reference lips, and text or marking recess placement. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon
steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 42 mm and length 37 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 14 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 24 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 14 mm wide over 29 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M7 holes through the top flat at X=12 mm and X=24 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 2 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: cross relief slot
functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 4 mm wide across the top flat at X=18 mm, depth 4 mm.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

requirements.part_name: Small Rotary Table Centering Plug - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Small Rotary Table Centering Plug'. The broader use
case is: Used to locate a workpiece or vise over the center bore of a small rotary table during
milling setups. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Stepped cylindrical plug
with taper lead-in, shoulder flange, threaded puller hole, and wrench flats. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Fits into the rotary table center hole and presents a raised pilot diameter for aligning
fixtures. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines coaxial steps,
taper transitions, shoulder references, internal threading representation, and flats intersecting
cylindrical bodies. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round bar
stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the
center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or
fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 72 mm and length 110 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 18 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 28 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 24 mm wide over 102 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M7 holes through the top flat at X=36 mm and X=73 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 6 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: opposed wrench flats
functional_features.description: Mill two opposed flats across the outside, leaving 64 mm across flats over the middle third of the length.

Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

source.domain: workshop fixtures and small machine-shop accessories
source.raw_idea_title: Vise Jaw Stop With Sliding Finger

requirements.part_name: Vise Jaw Stop With Sliding Finger - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Vise Jaw Stop With Sliding Finger'. The broader use
case is: Milling vise accessory that lets a machinist repeatedly position short workpieces against
the same side reference. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined clamp
body with jaw-grip hook, dovetail or slot for sliding finger, clamp screw bore, and stop face. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Clamps to a vise jaw and presents an adjustable stop finger that can be set
beside the cutting area. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests
nested functional features, clamping hooks, sliding interfaces, perpendicular stop faces, screw
bosses, and compact assembly-oriented geometry. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel,
normalized using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 205 mm x 45 mm x 30 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 6 mm on the length centerline at X=10 mm and X=195 mm, Y=22 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 68 mm long x 16 mm wide through the part, centered at X=102 mm, Y=22 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 51 mm x 18 mm x 8 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: dovetail slide form
functional_features.description: Machine a straight dovetail groove on the top face, length 181 mm, throat 12 mm, included angle 60 degrees.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 14 mm and undercut 5 mm for registration.

functional_features.name: side tapped hole
functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M9 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

source.raw_idea_title: Tap Guide Block With Interchangeable Bushings

requirements.part_name: Tap Guide Block With Interchangeable Bushings - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Tap Guide Block With Interchangeable Bushings'. The
broader use case is: Hand-tapping aid used on a drill press table or bench to keep taps square to a
drilled hole. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Rectangular guide block with
central counterbored bushing pocket, clearance window, side retention screw, and underside relief.
All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Guides a tap vertically through a close-fitting bushing while the
block sits flat on the workpiece. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Requires precise stacked bores, counterbores, replaceable insert pockets, side fastener geometry,
flatness-critical base features, and clearance cutouts. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon
steel, normalized using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as
needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
